equivalence_argument: The current SPC job reader has the required fields for existing jobs but does not skip zero-executor jobs as specified.

checked_queries: Missing job should return error for SPC verification
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: read_jobs_for_spc_verification returns an error for a zero executor slot instead of skipping that requested job id while returning entries for existing jobs.
related_but_insufficient_code: read_jobs_for_settlement skips zero-executor jobs, but read_jobs_for_spc_verification returns Err("Job ... not found") and its test asserts a missing job should return an error.

notes: Confirmed by the explicit zero-executor branch and test in ritual-async-registry.
rationale: The commit adds SpcVerificationJobData and a new read_jobs_for_spc_verification function whose documented purpose is to read the previous-block fields needed for SPC signature verification from AsyncJobTracker storage. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: read_jobs_for_spc_verification must return one SpcVerificationJobData entry per existing requested job, populated from AsyncJobTracker storage with commit_block, tx_hash, precompile_address, precompile_input, previous_block_hash, previous_block_number, previous_block_timestamp, and executor_address, while skipping job IDs whose executor slot is zero.
suggested_falsification_tests: Populate a mock revm Database with AsyncJobTracker storage for two job IDs, one with a nonzero executor and previous-block fields plus dynamic precompile input and one with a zero executor slot; call read_jobs_for_spc_verification and assert only the existing job is returned with every field matching storage.
summary: SPC job-context reads do not skip zero-executor job IDs.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call read_jobs_for_spc_verification with one existing job and one job whose executor slot is zero; the invariant expects one returned entry, but current code errors.
title: Registry exposes SPC job context
